an1.306:1.1: "I do not see, *bhikkhus*, any other single *dhamma* by which unarisen unwholesome *dhammas* arise, and arisen unwholesome *dhammas* increase and proliferate, as does wrong view, *bhikkhus*.
an1.306:1.2: For one with wrong view, *bhikkhus*, unarisen unwholesome *dhammas* arise, and arisen unwholesome *dhammas* increase and proliferate."
an1.307:1.1: "I do not see, *bhikkhus*, any other single *dhamma* by which unarisen wholesome *dhammas* arise, and arisen wholesome *dhammas* increase and proliferate, as does right view, *bhikkhus*.
an1.307:1.2: For one with right view, *bhikkhus*, unarisen wholesome *dhammas* arise, and arisen wholesome *dhammas* increase and proliferate."
an1.308:1.1: "I do not see, *bhikkhus*, any other single *dhamma* by which unarisen wholesome *dhammas* do not arise, and arisen wholesome *dhammas* decline, as does wrong view, *bhikkhus*.
an1.308:1.2: For one with wrong view, *bhikkhus*, unarisen wholesome *dhammas* do not arise, and arisen wholesome *dhammas* decline."
an1.309:1.1: "I do not see, *bhikkhus*, any other single *dhamma* by which unarisen unwholesome *dhammas* do not arise, and arisen unwholesome *dhammas* decline, as does right view, *bhikkhus*.
an1.309:1.2: For one with right view, *bhikkhus*, unarisen unwholesome *dhammas* do not arise, and arisen unwholesome *dhammas* decline."
an1.310:1.1: "I do not see, *bhikkhus*, any other single *dhamma* by which unarisen wrong view arises, and arisen wrong view grows, as does inappropriate attention [*ayonisomanasikāro*], *bhikkhus*.
an1.310:1.2: For one who attends inappropriately [*ayoniso*], *bhikkhus*, unarisen wrong view arises, and arisen wrong view grows."
an1.311:1.1: "I do not see, *bhikkhus*, any other single *dhamma* by which unarisen right view arises, and arisen right view grows, as does appropriate attention [*yoniso manasikāra*], *bhikkhus*.
an1.311:1.2: For one who attends appropriately [*yoniso*], *bhikkhus*, unarisen right view arises, and arisen right view grows."
an1.312:1.1: "I do not see, *bhikkhus*, any other single *dhamma* by which beings, at the breaking up of the body, after death, arise in a lower realm, a bad destination, a place of ruin, in hell, as does wrong view, *bhikkhus*.
an1.312:1.2: Beings endowed with wrong view, *bhikkhus*, at the breaking up of the body, after death, arise in a lower realm, a bad destination, a place of ruin, in hell."
an1.313:1.1: "I do not see, *bhikkhus*, any other single *dhamma* by which beings, at the breaking up of the body, after death, arise in a good destination, in a heavenly world, as does right view, *bhikkhus*.
an1.313:1.2: Beings endowed with right view, *bhikkhus*, at the breaking up of the body, after death, arise in a good destination, in a heavenly world."
an1.314:1.1: "For a person with wrong view, *bhikkhus*, whatever bodily *kamma* undertaken and carried out in accordance with that view, whatever verbal *kamma* … whatever mental *kamma* undertaken and carried out in accordance with that view — the intentions, the aspirations, the resolves, and the *saṅkhāras* — all those *dhammas* lead to what is unwanted, unpleasant, disagreeable, harmful, and to *dukkha*.
an1.314:1.4: Just as, *bhikkhus*, a neem seed, a bitter cucumber seed, or a bitter gourd seed, when planted in moist soil — whatever essence of the earth it draws up and whatever essence of the water it draws up — all of it turns to bitterness, pungency, and disagreeableness.
an1.314:1.7: In the same way, *bhikkhus*, for a person with wrong view, whatever bodily *kamma* undertaken and carried out in accordance with that view, whatever verbal *kamma* … whatever mental *kamma* undertaken and carried out in accordance with that view — the intentions, the aspirations, the resolves, and the *saṅkhāras* — all those *dhammas* lead to what is unwanted, unpleasant, disagreeable, harmful, and to *dukkha*.
an1.315:1.1: "For a person with right view, *bhikkhus*, whatever bodily *kamma* undertaken and carried out in accordance with that view, whatever verbal *kamma* … whatever mental *kamma* undertaken and carried out in accordance with that view — the intentions, the aspirations, the resolves, and the *saṅkhāras* — all those *dhammas* lead to what is wanted, pleasant, agreeable, beneficial, and to *sukha*.
an1.315:1.4: Just as, *bhikkhus*, a sugarcane seed, a rice seed, or a grape seed, when planted in moist soil — whatever essence of the earth it draws up and whatever essence of the water it draws up — all of it turns to sweetness, pleasantness, and natural deliciousness.
an1.315:1.7: In the same way, *bhikkhus*, for a person with right view, whatever bodily *kamma* undertaken and carried out in accordance with that view, whatever verbal *kamma* … whatever mental *kamma* undertaken and carried out in accordance with that view — the intentions, the aspirations, the resolves, and the *saṅkhāras* — all those *dhammas* lead to what is wanted, pleasant, agreeable, beneficial, and to *sukha*.
